heading: Grants for short-time compensation programs
label: 9028 Grants for short-time compensation programs
notes: References in TextSection 205 of the Federal-State Extended Unemployment Compensation Act of 1970, referred to in subsec. (i)(3), is , which is set out in a note under , Internal Revenue Code.section 205 of Pub. L. 91–373section 3304 of Title 26

children.heading: For implementation or improved administration
children.label: 1 For implementation or improved administration
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants to States that enact short-time compensation programs (as defined in subsection (i)(2)) for the purpose of implementation or improved administration of such programs.

children.heading: For promotion and enrollment
children.label: 2 For promotion and enrollment
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants to States that are eligible and submit plans for a grant under paragraph (1) for such States to promote and enroll employers in short-time compensation programs (as so defined).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine eligibility criteria for the grants under paragraphs (1) and (2).

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: B Clarification
children.text: A State administering a short-time compensation program that does not meet the definition of a short-time compensation program under , and a State with an agreement under , shall not be eligible to receive a grant under this section until such time as the State law of the State provides for payments under a short-time compensation program that meets such definition and such law.section 3306(v) of title 26section 9027 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The maximum amount available for making grants to a State under paragraphs (1) and (2) shall be equal to the amount obtained by multiplying $100,000,000 (less the amount used by the Secretary under subsection (e)) by the same ratio as would apply under subsection (a)(2)(B) of for purposes of determining such State’s share of any excess amount (as described in subsection (a)(1) of such section) that would have been subject to transfer to State accounts, as of , under the provisions of subsection (a) of such section.section 1103 of title 42October 1, 2019

children.label: A
children.text: one-third shall be available for a grant under subsection (a)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: two-thirds shall be available for a grant under subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Application
children.label: 1 Application
children.text: Any State seeking a grant under paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (a) shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and complete with such information as the Secretary may require. In no case may the Secretary award a grant under this section with respect to an application that is submitted after .December 31, 2023

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 2 Notice
children.text: The Secretary shall, within 30 days after receiving a complete application, notify the State agency of the State of the Secretary’s findings with respect to the requirements for a grant under paragraph (1) or (2) (or both) of subsection (a).

children.heading: Certification
children.label: 3 Certification
children.text: If the Secretary finds that the State law provisions meet the requirements for a grant under subsection (a), the Secretary shall thereupon make a certification to that effect to the Secretary of the Treasury, together with a certification as to the amount of the grant payment to be transferred to the State account in the Unemployment Trust Fund (as established in ) pursuant to that finding. The Secretary of the Treasury shall make the appropriate transfer to the State account within 7 days after receiving such certification.section 1104(a) of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: State law is not otherwise eligible for certification under or approvable under ; orsection 503 of title 42section 3304 of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: short-time compensation program is subject to discontinuation or is not scheduled to take effect within 12 months of the certification.
the creation or support of rapid response teams to advise employers about alternatives to layoffs;
the provision of education or assistance to employers to enable them to assess the feasibility of participating in short-time compensation programs; and

children.label: A
children.text: the submission and approval of plans; and

children.label: B
children.text: the filing and approval of new and ongoing short-time compensation claims.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: e Administration
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to use 0.25 percent of the funds available under subsection (g) to provide for outreach and to share best practices with respect to this section and short-time compensation programs.
terminated the State’s short-time compensation program; or
failed to meet appropriate requirements with respect to such program (as established by the Secretary).

children.heading: Funding
children.label: g Funding
children.text: There are appropriated, out of moneys in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, to the Secretary, $100,000,000 to carry out this section, to remain available without fiscal year limitation.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: h Reporting
children.text: The Secretary may establish reporting requirements for States receiving a grant under this section in order to provide oversight of grant funds.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 1 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Labor.

children.heading: Short-time compensation program
children.label: 2 Short-time compensation program
children.text: The term “short-time compensation program” has the meaning given such term in .section 3306(v) of title 26

children.heading: State; State agency; State law
children.label: 3 State; State agency; State law
children.text: The terms “State”, “State agency”, and “State law” have the meanings given those terms in section 205 of the Federal-State Extended Unemployment Compensation Act of 1970 ( note).26 U.S.C. 3304
